reviews.review_description: Best VR game pound for pound!!! Sooo much fun once you tweak settings to your preferences and get some practice in!

So the game card says single user. Multiplayer is active. It's a bit busted. Often it'll stop counting laps so one or the other of you will be racing infinitely and you'll have to restart app to clear it. Other than that it seems to 'work' fine. 
 The game itself rocks. It's brilliant. It's hard, but not impossible. The soundtrack is very appropriate although maybe a bit loud but we have volume controls so whatever.
 The controls. Yeah, have patience. It will come together. Id recommend trying linear controls on about .80, if the default isn't working for you. I had a better experience that way, more response near the center point and less unpredictable movement further out. Like I say I'm a beginner myself and I find that much better personally. 
 Evolution is due to release in march. This was £10.99, worth every penny. Despite the multiplayer having flaws it doesn't detract from the experience of ripping around a track buttering corners with your buddies, airborne, at 600kmh. Banger.
When I go to multiplayer, it dosent say any lobbies are avalible, idk if its a glitch or my wifi is bad, but otherwise, its an amazing game

reviews.review_title: Don't Give Up, It's Actually Good!
reviews.review_description: I rarely leave reviews, but for this, I had to. There is a learning curve. Do the training and the tutorial. I recommend sitting in a chair, and holding your control hand straight in front of you, not braced on anything. I tried resting both arms on the armrest, and I was terrible. As soon as I heled my hand straight out, the control started to feel more intuitive and natural. Don't move your whole arm, just your wrist and hand. The controls are actually very responsive, once you master it. Use your brakes! You can't just run through the track full throttle and expect not to slam into the wall. This game is fast! Like really fast. By far the best sense of speed and feeling immersed in a VR game I have experienced. The graphics are good, and the game runs surprisingly smooth.  I would be happy just flying through the tracks, but the racing is a great bonus for me. This could easily be a 5 star game however, there are a few persistent and frustrating bugs. Several times in just a few hours of play, after slamming into a wall, I glitched outside the map and it required a restart. An aggravation, yes, but the game is still fantastic.

This is what I've been waiting for. Excellent game. It's just like flying a plane. 
And it really is like Wipeout. 

I wish people played the real time multiplayer.
Hopefully soon, if word gets around about this game. ☺️
All in all I LOVE This GAME.  ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

People complaining about the controls, remember to hold the grip button and then it's fine. I can imagine many people forgetting the grip button and spinning/going everywhere then quitting and saying it's garbage. Once you grip the thing is actually quite exhilarating. Gonna keep and get better as it's a good game.
I beat the game i love the looks and just practice it people over react and crash out in 2 seconds just practice .
Controls are horrible. Every time I turn It it keeps going the same direction and I hit the wall. Wasted $15 for this game

The game seems well made, but with the controls being extremely awkward and ALL gameplay revolving around Multiplayer/ Online only (just like 'Exocars', another disappointment) minus the tutorial & Training lessons (I can'teven completethe Exam due tothe 3D controls being too much) - I just can't say this game is worth keeping for me personally even at sale price...

Should be great for those who can handle and have patience for complete 3D air piloting with 1 hand- and gesture only controls.
WHERE IS EVERYONE!!!!??!?

I used to play wipeout and waiting for something similar but in vr and for the quest headset,and here it is, very enjoyable game! First I read the reviews and thought I'm not getting this,the controls are bad, then today I bought it (my plan was to refund it if it's that bad) and wow it's a great game! Yes you need to practice, but after my let's say 10th try I finished first place on the first 3 tracks,so just keep practicing! Thank you for this awesome game!

reviews.review_title: I want to love this so badly…but I can't
reviews.review_description:
I was so excited when I saw this game on the store. I’m new to VR but I’ve been a zero-g racer since my PS one days with Wipeout, and my Xbox 360 days with quantum redshift. I was so excited when I saw this game. 

The aesthetic and the vehicles look amazing. There is a good tutorial which shows you how to calibrate your position and what the buttons do. Then there are lessons and exams to get you ready to race…. And that’s where it all goes wrong. 

As with so many other reviewers I have been plagued with control issues, mainly when using the tutorial levels. I’m a gamer and I have a very steady hand but I cannot make the ship go in a straight line in the tutorials. No matter how many times I calibrate, reposition, or adjust the sensitivity  it feels that as soon as I press the “grab” button which allows you to steer the ship my craft goes off in wild directions.

I was willing to invest the time to learn how to fly the ship and to learn the mechanics of the game. I thought the price was fair. I even hoped the other reviewers were simply lacking in fine motor control, but no.  

I hope the developers can repair the steering mechanic, until then this app is getting uninstalled.

Downloaded 4/3/25 on meta quest 3s

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We are working on an update for the game and will soon release an update with a new control type! Players will be able to choose the option that suits them best! We read all feedback and will do our best to make the game better for you!

reviews.review_description: Takes a while to get used to the airplane-like one hand joystick maneuvers but once you get it it's really fun. I'd wish the non dominant hand would be mobilized more tho, it's all about just one hand.

Amazing successor to the classic Wipe Out series, lots of tracks, the controls are super tight, but it takes some practice to get the hang of it.  The only real thing I would change is ship customization in the form of choosing new frames, wings, engines, adding engine trail lights.  Also, Amazon Music integration would be great, BUT I've been listening to a late 90s techno mix, including Future Sound of London, Daft Punk, Chemical Brothers, The Prodigy, Orbital, and man I just somehow channel my 1997 self and I'm in the zone 😂

This game would be more popular and perfect if the controllers were easier, they are very sensitive even if I lower the sensitivity setting, you will need at least an hour of practice to get used to it and start to enjoy. Developers, make the effort, don't abandon your creation, I really want this game to be successful, hear the feedback, you've made it so far, the game is so cool.

there is no meny button 
my ship keeps spinning uncontrollably by default, just spinning at a slightly tilted axis, which makes it impossible to fly 
 
i get glued into the walls when crashing, twice, cant finnish the race, since im stuck to the wall

and since there is no meny button, i cant do anything but restart the entire game every time, in order to get to the main menu

also cant redo the tutorial, it gets stuck at calibration, nothing happens when i press the left thumb stick 

since there is no menu button 

i cant go back to start menu

and must restart the entire game again 

pretty sure most people would have vomited their guts out after the ride i have just gone through 

ship just spins and does barrel rolls. . like youre brad pits dad in that space movie flying out into outer space to find aliens 

its totally unplayable, at least on my device

developer_response.body: Please email us and send a video of the issue, and we will do our best to assist you! info@xocus.co

reviews.review_description:
Genuinely feels like the direction is being selected at random. Really doesn’t matter what I do, the ship will suddenly tail off at any given moment. This was one of the most frustrating vr experiences I’ve had and I’m incredibly disappointed, this was a game I had high hopes for once I found it.

I can’t even get past the flight school because the ship is incapable of flying straight and is constantly breaking to slow me down even if I’m not braking.

Edit: what a worthless response. I did the tutorial, I do know how the controls work. They just suck. Why is the ship spinning if I don’t grip at all? Why does the ship constantly brake without pressing it? Why wouldn’t the flight school be the way to learn?

You clearly didn’t read my review at all and just decided to copy and paste some blurb.

“Just keep trying!” Yeah keep trying until you’ve passed the refund window! Then we get to keep your money even if you still hate the game!

developer_response.body:
You need to understand how control works.

We recommend learning on the first route.

Right Controller must be vertical 
this is the zero position. As soon as you press grep, ship control begins to work. The ship perceives any deviation from the zero position to turn.

Just give it time to master the controls and then you will enjoy the game.

Hundreds of users faced difficulties in mastering it from the beginning, but then wrote excellent reviews!

If you need help, write to us on discord and we will help.

reviews.review_description: Love this game. The tracks are fun and the controls (imo) are family initiative. Roll, yaw and pitch give you lots of freedom. The drifting aspect is a little too much however. And also, I am having fun playing against player's ghosts, but where's the 'realtime' multiplayer? There's an option for it but no one plays this from the looks of things. The tournament bit doesn't work either :/

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review. Right now all our small team are working on the new game EXOcars. It is our newest VR racing game.
If you liked Omega Pilot we will be happy to see you on the race in EXOcars!

reviews.review_title: This is what VR was made for
reviews.review_description: Wow!

Great game but how the heck to I unlock the other ships?? Also I wish I could toggle the control with grip instead of having to hold it down which hurts my hand after a while. Could definitely use Quest 3 optimizations but using QGO (if you have it) does make it a bit better.

Response to Devs: I still think having an option for players to toggle on and off the ship controls and also explicitly stating how to unlock everything (within the game) would make this a 5 star game. Add updated quest 3 graphics and it's a 6 star game!! :)

developer_response.body: Hey there! Thanks for the kind words. Regarding ship customization, it depends on the number and type of medals you receive on the tracks. Also, try turning off vibrations in the settings, and your hand won't get hurt anymore!

reviews.review_title: I don’t know what we have here
reviews.review_description:
The graphics are just HORRIBLE!
The controllers are MADDENING!

But I have to recommend this to anyone looking for a thrill. Because it’s thrilling when you know what you’re doing.

I knew what I was doing for the first few stages. Once you have a flow going it’s outrageously fun. However my flow stopped significantly early on and I didn’t have it in me to master the controls, which by the way make sense for sure. Don’t blame the developers because I think mastering the control mechanics is what this game is all about.

Really weird levels with random subjects. They look terrible. 

If you want to give this a go, use this link below. I only give honest reviews.

Change comma to period 

L I N K T R , 
                    E E
                          /
                            D I S C O U N T
                                                  F U R Y

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! Mastering controls is truly the key to enjoying the gameplay, you're right:) We strived to provide comfortable training and Pilot school that helps players to understand the basics and more. We are glad that you liked the game more, there will be updates in the future and we hope that you enjoy them!

reviews.review_description:
Omega Pilot is a virtual reality racing game that reminds me very much of the old school Super Nintendo game F-Zero. This game makes you feel like you are racing a fighter jet with controls of the distant future. The game has amazing graphics and sound effects. The music also keeps the adrenaline pumping while you race your way through several massive tracks. The controls are a bit hard to master but once you do, it feels extremely rewarding. This game is totally worth a download if you are into racing. Be careful though, your VR legs will be tested with all the twists and turns of this game!

Video Review: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vCCZNf9dzTA

developer_response.body: Thank you for your video review and such a kind feedback! We work for you:)

reviews.review_description:
With the latest update the game has received a very solid polish pass over the entire game as well as adding new content. Since launching on app lab the game has only got better and better, with the latest maps really maturing in design.

For newcomers be warned this game does has a steep learning curve, but it is short and well worth the time invested to get a feel of the controls. Best played in a comfy chair with the Quest 2 controllers rested on your leg/knee and treated like a HOTAS. Keep your vision focused on the upcoming track turns, and after getting comfortable with the sensitivity of the controls you won’t flip much until you really start to push lap times. With the latest update there are now sensitivity options which make finding a good balance easy.

The soundtrack, visuals and design are top notch for Quest 2 racing games on the platform, both app lab and the main store. If you want a good value for money game where you can play for dozens of hours then this game will satisfy you. This really isn’t an “experience” VR app and a fully fledge game. The gameplay loop of improving your times and racing against other players around your pace really keeps you in the headset. I have completely lost hours in this game when playing plugged into power. The game ‘s soundtrack really really slaps, but it is also a great game to play with the music off while listening to podcasts.

Hopefully Meta take note of this game and promote it to the main store, racing fans are missing out on this Quest 2 hidden gem!

To me this would be worth buying the headset for my most favourite game was all the incarnations of wipeout so this a dreamer come true. But there's allways butt's. Different ships like in the real wipeout. And some truly sick inducing tracks. Just like wipeout 2097.and those tunes. Xx

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for such a kind feedback, we're glad you enjoy our game!

reviews.review_description: Love the speed of the Wipeout clone and the control method, my only gripe is no left hand control. Also a restart course button would be useful as sometimes I got stuck and no way to get unstuck except to restart the game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! Great news for you, we added pause buttons and left hand control 
We'll be grateful if you improve your rating:)

reviews.review_title: Pretty good. Just needs some updates
reviews.review_description:
This is a really enjoyable game with great visuals. I never felt dizzy or got motion sickness so I highly recommend people try it out even if they’re afraid of that. The controls are fine as well, though I’m still getting the hang of it. Found it easier to play than the original Wipeout game. The tracks look nice and varied with quite a number of them already with more to come.

It does have some minor to  moderate negatives. Firstly, not a fan of the challenge modes especially since we have to do them to unlock the next race tracks. If they were a separate mode It would be fine. I would’ve liked if every track had a practice option before you start. Almost every racing game I’ve played had them.

Pros - Visuals 
           Controls
           Gameplay
           Comfortable
           Lots of tracks with more to come

Cons - Forced challenge modes
             No practice modes
             Still needs updates to feel complete
             Some bugs need patching

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you for your feedback:)
We added control settings so now you can try it again, hope it will become more comfortable for you to play and you will improve your rating!

reviews.review_description:
Lots of tracks, great sense of speed, for now just asynchronous multiplayer, but the live one is coming sooner or later as i heard, so i will trust devs and give 5 stars :P

Edit: Bought the game hearing promises that live multiplayer is coming, tho doesnt seem that way. No way to even race with friend's ghost, i'm not satisfied, probably wouldn't make that purchase knowing what i know today.

Edit2: i can see that real time multiplayer is finally here. Now i just need to find somebody to test it :) But its nice to see game going in good direction. Fixed my rating

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your update! We will add an in-game banner to let you know that real-time multiplayer is active on Tuesday and Saturday at 17:00 UTC 
Join our Discord channel, there you can chat with other players and play together! https://discord.gg/eygm2D83

reviews.review_description:
Finally got the Quest version, even if i already own it on Steam! 
Omega Pilot is definitely one of my favorite VR games, and easily the best racing game on Quest! The motion controls may be weird at first, but are in my opinion one of the most intuitive and rewarding control schemes. 

Also i am really fond of the new endurance mode. Perfect for Youtube-Gameplay! ;)

The only complaint i have at the moment is that the 5 new tracks are a little uninspired and the invisible walls feel really weird and restictive.
Melange in particular is a mess. There is no clear direction in the design of the track and you constantly crash into an invisible wall which just feels like being there. IDK it just feels rushed to me.

But still this game brought me hours of fun on Steam and i hope for even more hours here on Quest 2! 

Cheers!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot fir such a kind words and detailed feedback! We're glad to hear that Omega Pilot brings you joy. Also we will add multiplayer soon so you can race with your mates and feel more fun:)

reviews.review_description: Very good more variety of ships please take inspiration from wipeout omega maybe even power ups or something as a separate mode I really like the current version of asynchronous racing just more modes and better variety customization for vehicles like colors parts engine parts etc and track optimization I feel like I hit mor walls then I should some courses don’t feel ship sized so to speak I believe Tokyo track for example try to double up an track size real tracks are huge and multi lane not a single street with sharp turns especially for the kind of vehicle and the way it handles very good or all just some add ons and optimal sized tracks would be amazing oh and tracks should just be unlocked like mortal combat so I can just race how I want I must have gave the second course 50 tries before I could even look at the next track

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your feedback and high rating! We listen to every suggestion and constantly improve Omega Pilot.

reviews.review_description: purchasesd this game and it froze on me after tutorials. have uninstall and reinstalld couple of times with no luck... pls help

developer_response.body: Thank you! We will check it asap and fix the issue, keep you posted

reviews.review_description:
This game is super fun until some point, the game has high graphics, ok controls, and fun maps, up until the dirt road, almost impossible for you to get first and or get the bronze score, I only lucked out and got put in a noob lobby on my 10TH TRY ON THAT ONE MAP, then the next one after that wasn’t too bad, but then the arctic map was just bad, track is wayyy to small to break in time, there is no wall in the track so you go flying out a good distance until a walk FINALLY appears, and going gently is no way to beat it because the time to win is ONLY 1.50.00 MINUTES you have yo luck out and get in a noob lobby but it will take awhile to do so, then is every map if you hit the roof, your hovercraft is stuck on the roof for about 1.5 seconds, might not seem like a lot, but then you realize you loss a lot of speed in that little time, this is apparent in Olympus, with the small hole your very likely to get stuck,

In short, this game is fun until the dirt road and you get stuck to roof in every map if not careful

developer_response.body: Thank you for such detailed feedback! We want each player to enjoy Omega Pilot so we will rely on your experience to improve our game in the future. For now I can offer to open all the tracks for you so that you can play with pleasure! Please email us your in-game nickname info@xocus.co

reviews.review_description:
Ok. Stars added for sticks control. Didn’t try it yet but hope for the best.

VR needs more races obviously, so I hope developers will improve the game. Would be good to have possibility to control with a sticks or at least have the virtual yoke or something for the visual reference. Now controls are not comfortable.

Once I see the sticks controls - I'll add 2 stars )

developer_response.body: Thank you very much!

reviews.review_description: You need some time to get used to the controls, but after that its just so satisfying to fly through all the tracks. Some of them are just mind blowing. Totally recommend this game!

developer_response.body:
We're so grateful for your feedback! Glad to know you enjoy Omega Pilot:) 
Thank you and stay tuned, new track coming soon

reviews.review_description:
I just bought the game, yes really clear and bringing good wipeout vibes, but please make an update that we can change to stick controll the ship left right up and down ….

I write to the Developer and now Stick Controll 😃 thats absolute fantastic and thx to the Developer now 5 Stars … thx thats how gameing works today …

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! We will continue to improve the game based on players feedback:)

reviews.review_description: Great game, it feels like drone racing 😁

developer_response.body: Thank you for your nice feedback!

reviews.review_description:
25% OFF CODE: delete the space between “.com”

https://www.oculus. com/appreferrals/PowPow55/5222048931152033/?utm_source=2

This would be a great 5 star game if it were easier to learn. The controls are great but really difficult to master. That said, once you do learn the controls, it becomes FZero VR. Who can say no to that?

I’m glad I got this game in the lab phase. I see the potential and can’t wait till it comes off the lab. I still have a fun time racing. It is challenging because I feel like I’m racing in a dream where I’m just not in total control, however the potential I see is very promising. I hope there will be some multiplayer races when fully developed. If your into racing get on board and start practicing now. This game is going to be extremely awesome. Let’s go guys let’s get this game going.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your feedback! Already this summer we plan to add realtime multiplayer and now working on its implementation. And in a week we will release a Major update which will include a new racing mode! Hope you will enjoy it and rate us higher:)

reviews.review_description:
A stunning racing game that fully deserves its 5-star rating. However, I have some suggestions to share. It's a bit confusing not to be required to go through the tutorial before starting the game. As a player on the Quest 3, I immediately dived into the game without going through the tutorial, which made me doubt its compatibility with my headset. To enhance this experience, it would be beneficial for the tutorial to launch automatically upon opening the game, providing better guidance for new players.

Furthermore, I was disappointed to find no tournaments available during my gaming session. Incorporating more frequent or regular tournaments would add a stimulating competitive dimension to the game.

In terms of innovation, I suggest adding an 'Arena' mode where players can compete in a setting similar to a football match. A large arena, two teams of 5 or 3, piloting their vehicles while trying to score goals. This could bring a unique gaming dynamic and enhance the game's appeal.

Lastly, to maintain player engagement, the addition of new circuits would be greatly appreciated. Congratulations to the team behind this outstanding game🥰🙏

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much! You have a lot of useful suggestions for us, we appreciate it a lot. We will definitely take them into account! Our team is very glad that you enjoy Omega Pilot:)  
New track will be released soon so please stay tuned

reviews.review_description: This hame has great controlles, awsome tracks, fun custimizatiouns and much more. I cant wait to see whats next in this game

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your nice feedback! We are glad that you get a pleasant experience from our game

reviews.review_description:
I've been a big fan of Wipeout style games since the Playstation days, even playing original Wipeout via emulation. No idea about F-Zero mentioned in other reviews as I haven't played it but this really does satisfy my cravings. What I have played on PCVR though is BallisticNG which for anyone that doesn't know is an amazing  PCVR Wipeout clone. What the devs have pulled off here, to get a game like this on standalone Quest is amazing. The variety of tracks with the frequent updates, the improved controls, the feeling of pure fast paced action!  It all adds up to one incredible game.
Some people mention getting sick when flying, I've not had problems, whether that's because I'm more used to VR in general or because there's plenty of options to reduce that in game I'm not sure, but this is fast paced flying,  it might be that some people just need to build up their tolerance for this type of game.

Negatives- the  pause feature, can accidentally be  triggered if the ship is set up in a way your hands can trigger it while flying and cost you a race 

I get that unlocking courses by coming in the top three encourages you to "get good" but  having zero customisation options even when you're unlocking a dozen tracks is a little disheartening. I don't want to be stuck with the same colour! Can't you consider giving us some more ship customisation options related to course unlocking instead of just basing it on times?
Edit: revisited earlier tracks after adjusting controls and got times down further & managed to unlock some, I think it's still hard for new players.
Bug makes your ship sometimes fly off the course with no way back  and you have to press the exit track option! Maybe I should slow down :P

Aside from that once you get the sensitivity controls worked out to your own preference and start making those tight turns this is one hell of a game and I love it!
My Gameplay an hour in back in June:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z-bBNd7KoJY
